description: Conversational, IMPLICIT-INTENT tool-chain scenarios. The user never names the tools to call; the correct chain (3-5 sequential calls with data handoff) must be inferred from intent that builds across turns. DATA ONLY. PII-free by construction (abstract place/person names, no IPs, no CT numbers, no secrets). The S83 EXPLICIT agent scenarios are referenced by ID via `s83_reference_ids` below and loaded from agent-scenarios.json at runtime — they are NOT copied into this file.
s83_reference_note: The explicit-task chains for dim-2 are the S83 multi_step scenarios. We reference them by id (no bodies duplicated). The dim-2 runner loads them from the S83 corpus where present and only ADDS the conversational scenarios below.

scenarios.intent_summary: User is implicitly preparing for a weekend trip mentioned across two turns; correct behavior is to look up the destination weather, then check the calendar for the trip dates, then set a packing reminder — without ever being told which tools to call.
turns: I'm heading to Zubrovka this weekend to see my sister.
I always forget to pack for the weather there. Can you make sure I'm sorted before I leave?
scenarios.notes: Implicit chain weather -> calendar -> reminder. The destination 'Zubrovka' planted in turn 1 must flow into the weather lookup and the reminder text (data handoff). No tool is named by the user.

scenarios.intent_summary: User implies they want to know whether to leave early for their first meeting; correct behavior is to read today's calendar for the first meeting, estimate the current commute, then set a departure reminder — intent implied, tools not stated.
turns: My first thing this morning is across town and I hate being late.
Traffic's been awful lately. Just handle it so I leave on time, would you?
scenarios.notes: Implicit chain calendar -> commute -> reminder. The first-meeting time from the calendar feeds the commute math, which feeds the departure reminder. User says 'just handle it' — never names a tool.

scenarios.intent_summary: User implies they want to cook a specific dinner but may be missing ingredients; correct behavior is to list the pantry, add the missing items to the shopping list, then set a reminder to shop — three sequential calls with the missing-items handoff, no tools named.
turns: I want to make that big pasta dinner for everyone on Friday.
Whatever I don't already have, make sure it ends up on my list and I don't forget to grab it.
Perfect, and nudge me about it before the weekend.
scenarios.notes: Implicit chain pantry -> shopping_list -> reminder. The pantry gap drives what is written to the shopping list, which drives the reminder. Three turns, intent built up; the dish 'pasta' is the planted handoff token.
